title: Fix API: Vercel preset + restore CORS and root health after Nitro migration
description: Production api.shipradar.dev hangs (0 bytes, times out). Nitro builds preset=node-server instead of vercel preset, so Vercel has no function to serve. Force preset=vercel in nitro.config.ts. Also restore CORS middleware and GET / health route dropped/moved during the Nitro migration.

title: Fix API admin routes
description: Repair apps/api admin routes without restoring Better Auth sessions/verifications to Postgres. Remove the stale sessions dependency from admin activity, preserve admin route response contracts, and verify the API package.

title: Complete Polar teardown: remove orphaned billing/Pro UI
description: Prior commit removed the Polar plugin+deps but left web UI calling removed Polar methods (checkout/customer.state/customer.portal), now broken. Full teardown: delete all billing/Pro UI (pricing page, pricing components, subscription section, useUserTier hook, upgrade-prompt, pro-badge), edit settings/section.tsx and user-menu.tsx, strip Polar from pricing-tiers skill, remove leaked POLAR_* secrets from packages/database/.env.

title: Migrate apps/web from shadcn/ui to HeroUI Pro/OSS
description: Completely remove shadcn/ui (24 primitives, 36 consumers, ~15 Radix deps) and rebuild every dashboard/admin/auth surface on HeroUI v3 + Pro, applying HeroUI Pro design taste. Phased: 1) dashboard core (AppLayout+Sidebar+nav), 2) dashboard feature components, 3) tables->DataGrid, 4) pricing+marketing audit, 5) teardown+docs. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/apps-web-completely-remove-shadcn-ui-smooth-liskov.md

title: Remove Polar integration
description: Remove Polar-related dependencies, auth plugins, MCP configuration, skills, and project references because the current Polar setup is breaking the project.

title: Investigate authenticated API route timeout in production
description: Production API requests were timing out on https://api.shipradar.dev before authenticated routes were disabled. A temporary isolation branch test/disable-authenticated-api-routes at commit abea7f7 comments out dashboard, repos, and admin route wiring in apps/api/src/index.ts. After that deploy, public endpoints respond: GET / returns 200 {status: ok}, GET /health returns 200, GET /stats returns 200, and GET /repos returns 404 as expected because authenticated routes are disabled. This suggests the timeout is caused by authenticated route wiring or dependencies rather than Nitro/Hono base routing. Suspects include jwtAuth fetching JWKS_URL without an AbortSignal/timeout, Upstash Redis calls in route/cache helpers, DATABASE_URL/Neon calls, GitHub API calls, or package root import side effects from @shipradar/database re-exporting auth. Fix later by re-enabling authenticated routes incrementally, adding bounded timeouts/fail-fast env validation, and avoiding accidental auth.ts evaluation from API DB imports.
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated routes can be re-enabled without causing https://api.shipradar.dev to timeout; missing or unreachable JWKS_URL fails fast with a clear 5xx/401 response instead of hanging; API imports do not require unrelated Better Auth/Polar/OAuth secrets; root / and /health remain fast; production verification documents which dependency caused the timeout.

title: Restore broken apps/api routes after Nitro migration
description: Only GET /health is live; all other routes commented out in src/index.ts. Root cause: @api/* alias unresolved by Nitro rolldown. Add Nitro alias + tsconfig baseUrl/paths, re-enable all HTTP routes except internal/workflow/cron.

title: Migrate API runtime to Vercel
description: Convert apps/api from Cloudflare Workers runtime assumptions to a Vercel-hosted standalone Hono API. Keep Better Auth and Neon, replace Cloudflare KV/rate limiting/AI/runtime bindings with Vercel-compatible services, and pause after apps/api changes for review.

title: Handle missing Telegram chat IDs in release workflow
description: Release workflow can fail when Telegram channel KV records are missing chatId even though chatId may still exist in telegram:{chatId} mappings. Add defensive recovery and cleanup so missing chatId records do not break workflow execution.
close_reason: Implemented workflow recovery and cleanup for missing Telegram chat IDs

title: Handle missing Telegram chat IDs in release workflow
description: Release workflow can fail when a DB-backed Telegram channel config is missing chatId even though CHANNELS KV may still contain telegram:{chatId} -> userId mappings. Add defensive handling and fallback lookup so missing channel config chatId does not fail workflow steps.

title: Reduce release check workflow subrequests
description: The release check workflow can exceed Cloudflare's per-invocation subrequest limit because it fans out across all repos and repeats KV/DO calls inside nested repo/chat loops. Batch processing and aggregate repeated calls so scheduled and manual checks stay below Worker limits.
close_reason: Implemented batching and aggregated release check workflow subrequests

title: Add Email notification channel
description: Add a verified email notification channel for release updates. Use Resend or an equivalent Worker-compatible sender, add channel config/types/routes, implement verification before activation, and surface it in the integrations UI as a coming-soon/then active channel.
acceptance_criteria: Users can add an email address as a notification channel; email must be verified before notifications are sent; release summaries are formatted for email; users can enable, disable, and remove the channel; tests or typechecks cover the new channel types.

title: Wire Discord into release notifications
description: Refactor the release-check notification path so Discord channels receive release notifications through the existing multi-channel notifyUser abstraction instead of the workflow sending only Telegram messages. Preserve Telegram behavior and failure isolation between channels.
acceptance_criteria: Discord channels receive release notifications for tracked repos; Telegram notifications continue to work; notification failures are isolated per channel; workflow uses the shared notification service rather than direct Telegram-only sending.

title: Migrate dashboard shell to HeroUI Pro AppLayout
description:
GitHub: https://github.com/ruchernchong/release-watch/issues/43

Replace the shadcn dashboard shell with HeroUI Pro layout primitives.

Acceptance Criteria:

- Sidebar collapse/mobile behavior works without shadcn SidebarProvider.

Prepare the web app for HeroUI Pro by loading required CSS and resolving theme token compatibility.

title: Plan migration from shadcn/ui to HeroUI Pro

GitHub: https://github.com/ruchernchong/release-watch/issues/41

Track the migration of the web app from generated shadcn/ui components to HeroUI Pro and HeroUI v3 components.

Scope:

- Replace the dashboard shell with HeroUI Pro AppLayout, Navbar, and Sidebar.

Acceptance Criteria:

title: [bug] bd dep add fails in embedded mode — 'depends_on_id' column not found
description:
bd dep add is broken in this workspace (embedded Dolt mode), so dependency edges cannot be created.

Repro:
  bd dep add release-watch-k9p release-watch-edm
Result:
  Error: failed to check for dependency cycle: Error 1105: column "depends_on_id" could not be found in any table in scope
  (exit 1)

Environment:
  bd version 1.0.4 (ce242a879: main@ce242a879678)
  Embedded Dolt mode (.beads/embeddeddolt). 'bd doctor' is also unsupported in embedded mode.

Impact: no formal dependency edges can be added. Worked around release-watch-k9p -> release-watch-edm by recording the linkage in both issues' notes instead.

Likely cause: the dependency-cycle check query references a 'depends_on_id' column that doesn't exist in the embedded-mode schema (schema drift between bd version and the embedded Dolt DB).

Possible fixes to try: bump bd version, 'bd init --server' (switch to server mode), or migrate/reinitialize the embedded Dolt schema. Once resolved, run: bd dep add release-watch-k9p release-watch-edm

title: Consolidate apps/api into apps/web and drop the JWT/JWKS bridge
description:
Fold the Hono API (apps/api) into the Next.js app (apps/web), retiring the separate shipradar-api Vercel project and the cross-service JWT auth.

Scope: move Telegram webhook -> a Next.js route handler (runtime=nodejs) + re-point setWebhook; release-check cron -> Vercel cron hitting a route handler guarded by CRON_SECRET; REST data routes -> Server Components/lib/data + Server Actions (stats/admin already done); notifications/AI/Octokit/Discord/Upstash are plain Node libs that move as-is. Drop middleware/auth.ts JWKS verification and the disabled api-client/RPC layer; use getSession() directly.

Known risk to verify first: the release-check uses Vercel Workflows ('use workflow') — confirm it behaves the same inside Next.js (and maxDuration). Currently dormant anyway.

Unblocked by the no-mobile-app decision (release-watch-edm). Should resolve the two-service symptom in release-watch-h8t (authenticated API route timeout). Touches the release-watch-yod Telegram-auth epic.
notes: Depends on / unblocked by release-watch-edm (no-mobile-app decision). Related: release-watch-h8t (two-service auth timeout this would resolve), release-watch-yod (Telegram auth epic). NOTE: formal 'bd dep add' currently fails in embedded mode (schema bug: depends_on_id column not found) — dependency recorded here in notes instead.

title: Gate Sentry to production-only via VERCEL_ENV + env DSN
description: Disable Sentry in local dev and preview deploys. Read DSN from NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN instead of hardcoding, and only call Sentry.init when running in production (VERCEL_ENV/NEXT_PUBLIC_VERCEL_ENV === 'production'). Applies to sentry.server.config.ts, sentry.edge.config.ts, instrumentation-client.ts, and expose NEXT_PUBLIC_VERCEL_ENV via next.config.ts.

title: Remove Polar, keep subscriptions inert for future Stripe
description: Polar billing removed in 40f9850. Restore staged-deleted subscription/pricing UI, comment out Polar-coupled authClient.checkout/customer calls with TODO(stripe), hide upgrade/checkout/manage CTAs, and clean stray Polar refs in .env and pricing-tiers SKILL.md.

title: Redesign marketing pages with HeroUI Pro (Glass)
description: Migrate all 4 (marketing) pages (home, pricing, privacy, terms) from shadcn/ui to HeroUI Pro + OSS components using the Glass theme. Add tasteful motion to landing page. Extract pricing-data.ts. See ~/.claude/plans/i-want-to-do-lucky-goose.md

title: Investigate Next.js build hang after route output
description: After the ShipRadar rename, both pnpm build via Turborepo and pnpm --filter @shipradar/web build reached Next.js route output/finalization but did not exit before shell timeouts, eventually receiving SIGINT/SIGTERM (exit 130/143). Determine whether this is an existing Next.js/Turbopack/Sentry process-lifecycle issue and fix or document the required build invocation.

title: Rename project to ShipRadar
description: Rename package scope, product branding, domains, and documentation from ReleaseWatch/release-watch/@release-watch to ShipRadar/shipradar/@shipradar as requested.
close_reason: Completed ShipRadar rename across package scope, branding, domains, docs, and source references; filed release-watch-8ad for the unrelated build process hang.

title: Integrate Cloudflare AI Gateway
description: Route the existing Workers AI release analysis calls through Cloudflare AI Gateway while preserving current behavior when no gateway is configured.
close_reason: Integrated Cloudflare AI Gateway routing for release analysis

title: Route bot repo-add preview through shared fallback formatter
description: The 'Now tracking → latest release' preview (bot/index.ts:474) uses a duplicate formatLatestRelease() with raw substring(0,500) truncation and an unescaped href, reproducing the wall-of-text/mid-word bug already fixed in telegram.service.ts. Reuse formatFallbackReleaseNotes + escape helpers so both HTML send paths render identically.
close_reason: Routed bot repo-add preview (formatLatestRelease) through shared formatFallbackReleaseNotes + escapeHtml/escapeHtmlAttribute; removed duplicate escapeHtml and raw substring(0,500) truncation. tsc + biome clean.

title: AI release summary returns null for large changelogs → always hits fallback
description: next.js canary notifications (and likely other large changelogs) render the AI-less fallback: 📦 unknown category + no Highlights section means analyzeRelease() returned null. Model ID @cf/google/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it is valid, so likely cause is max_tokens:300 (ai.service.ts:95) being too small to emit valid strict-schema JSON for big release notes, causing a parse failure. Investigate raising max_tokens and/or relaxing strict JSON, verify with wrangler dev against a real next.js canary release.

title: Fix Telegram fallback release-notes formatting
description: AI-less fallback notifications render as a run-on wall of text with mid-word truncation. Rewrite formatFallbackReleaseNotes in apps/api/src/services/telegram.service.ts to render bold section headings, one bullet per changelog line, an '…and N more' overflow line, and word-boundary truncation. Move HTML escaping inside the function.
close_reason: Rewrote formatFallbackReleaseNotes to render bold headings + one bullet per line + …and N more overflow + word-boundary truncation; moved HTML escaping inside the function. Verified against real next.js canary body. Typecheck + lint clean.

title: Update integrations UI with notification channel states
description: Update the integrations UI to present notification channels by status: Telegram and Discord as active, Email/Slack/Webhooks/RSS/X as Coming soon, and Pushover as Planned. Ensure the display sets correct expectations without implying unavailable channels are connected.
acceptance_criteria: Integrations UI shows Telegram and Discord as active channels; Email, Slack, Webhooks, RSS, and X display Coming soon; Pushover displays Planned; unavailable channels cannot be configured yet; copy is clear on mobile and desktop.

title: Add Webhook notification channel
description: Add outbound webhook notifications for automation users and Pro-tier workflows. Define a versioned payload, signing strategy, retry behavior, and UI/API for managing webhook endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: Users can register webhook endpoints; notifications send a documented versioned payload; requests are signed with a per-webhook secret; users can enable, disable, and remove webhooks; tier limits are enforced when applicable.

title: Add Slack notification channel
description: Add Slack release notifications, starting with an incoming webhook MVP. Store Slack channel configuration, send formatted release summaries through Slack webhooks, and add UI for connecting and managing Slack channels.
acceptance_criteria: Users can add a Slack webhook channel; release summaries are delivered to Slack; users can enable, disable, and remove Slack channels; invalid webhooks are rejected or reported clearly.

title: Define technical design for Telegram + Better Auth integration
description: Research Telegram OAuth/Login Widget and Mini App constraints, then document required auth flow, claim validation, and API/provider gaps before implementation.

title: Implement Telegram custom Better Auth provider/plugin prototype
description: Build a proof-of-concept Telegram connection flow (custom provider/plugin) in the app, including auth callback handling and user profile mapping placeholders.

title: Research migration of apps/api to Vercel
description: Investigate feasibility, required refactors, and execution plan for migrating the apps/api package (currently Cloudflare Worker-based) to Vercel. Confirm runtime compatibility, env/secret handling, routing model, build and test process, and deployment workflow changes; produce a short migration plan with risks, effort estimate, and follow-on implementation tasks.
notes:
Findings update:
- Vercel migration is feasible but not a low-risk lift.
- apps/api is deeply coupled to Cloudflare: Wrangler entrypoints/workflows/cron, KV, Durable Objects, AI, Rate Limit, and cloudflare:workers runtime types.
- Significant coupling in src/index.ts (Sentry.withSentry + ExportedHandler + scheduled handler), src/workflows/release-check.ts (WorkflowEntrypoint), src/durable-objects/stats.ts (DurableObject), wrangler.jsonc triggers/workflows/rate limits, and Env typegen.
- Non-trivial replacement set required:
  1) Worker cron + workflow => Vercel Cron + background job/queue (likely Vercel Cron + Redis/Upstash or DB-driven scheduler + BullMQ/Temporal).
  2) KV namespaces + list/put/get/delete + TTL + prefix scans => choose Redis/Upstash or DB tables.
  3) Durable Object stateful counters => Postgres row counters / row-level atomic ops.
  4) Cloudflare RateLimit binding => Vercel Edge Middleware/Ratelimit (e.g., Upstash Redis + middleware) or service-level enforcement.
  5) Cloudflare AI @cf model binding => external model provider (OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini) + new key handling.
  6)  fetch options in JWKS caching => remove Cloudflare-specific cache hint and use generic cache layer.
  7) Cloudflare Sentry SDK + types => use @sentry/node or @sentry/vercel-edge depending on adapter.
  8) wrangler scripts/dependencies and Cloudflare types => replaced with Vercel build/deploy scripts and @types/node runtime types.
- Recommendation: migration not better
Further note: recommendation is no migration unless Vercel-specific constraints are required (team tooling, org standards, or platform dependencies), because Cloudflare-native features already map well to this workload.
Decision: keep on Cloudflare unless a strong Vercel requirement appears. A migration would likely be 2-4 weeks for one engineer due workflow storage refactors, plus another week for parity testing and rollback safety.
User preference update: proceed with Vercel destination. Next step is migration execution plan rather than stay-on-CF recommendation. Keep web on Vercel and move API runtime to Vercel-compatible stack with behavior parity plan.
Decision lock: migrate apps/api to Vercel platform (paired with apps/web on Vercel). Treat prior keep-on-CF conclusion as pre-migration analysis only; final path is Vercel execution plan.

title: Research migration of @apps/api to Vercel
description: Investigate feasibility, required refactors, and execution plan for migrating the  service (currently Cloudflare Worker based) to Vercel. Confirm runtime compatibility, environment variable/secret handling, routing model, build/test process, and deployment workflow changes; deliver a short migration plan with risks, effort estimate, and next implementation ticket(s).
close_reason: Superseded by corrected issue release-watch-lxy with full details

title: Add a Preview smoke test for AI summaries
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
It is not obvious how to quickly verify that AI-generated release summaries work in a Vercel Preview deployment without triggering the full release-check workflow. The existing dashboard display reads cached summaries from Redis, while the workflow path can process real tracked repositories and potentially send notifications depending on the Preview environment configuration.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add a Preview-only smoke test path for AI summary generation. The endpoint should call the existing web AI summary service with canned release notes and return the generated structured summary, category, breaking-change flag, and highlights.

The route should be unavailable in production and protected with an existing secret, such as the cron bearer token, so it can be tested safely from a Preview URL with curl.

Suggested shape:

```bash
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $CRON_SECRET" \
  https://<preview-url>/api/debug/ai-summary
```

The test should verify the actual Vercel Preview runtime path for the AI SDK, Vercel AI Gateway/OIDC credentials, and schema/object generation.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Triggering the existing `/api/cron/start-check` route on Preview is closer to end-to-end, but it may touch real DB, Redis, tracked repos, Telegram channels, or notification state if Preview is not fully isolated.

Manually inserting a cached `release:{repo}:{tag}` Redis value would verify the dashboard display, but it would not prove the AI summary generation path works on Vercel Preview.

**Additional context**
Current implementation notes:

- `apps/web/src/services/ai.service.ts` uses the AI SDK with `gateway("openai/gpt-5-nano")`.
- `apps/web/src/app/api/dashboard/releases/route.ts` reads cached analysis through `getCachedAnalysis`.
- `apps/web/src/workflows/release-check.ts` generates and stores summaries through the workflow path.
- The smoke endpoint should avoid sending Telegram notifications or mutating release notification state.

title: Evaluate migrating apps/web (Next.js 16) to Cloudflare Workers via @opennextjs/cloudflare
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
`apps/web` (Next.js 16) runs on Vercel; `apps/api` runs on Cloudflare Workers. Consolidating the web app onto Cloudflare would put the whole stack on one platform and make the SST proposal in #22 strictly more valuable. The main concern is whether the Next.js bundle fits under the Cloudflare Workers 10 MB gzipped Paid-plan limit.

**Proposed approach**
Use [`@opennextjs/cloudflare`](https://opennext.js.org/cloudflare) to adapt the Next.js build output into a Worker with `nodejs_compat`. BetterAuth, Neon HTTP driver, route handlers, Server Actions, and `proxy.ts` should all run unchanged. Workers Assets handles static files and fonts.

**Current Next.js feature usage**
- App Router + Server Components + Server Actions + `proxy.ts`
- `reactCompiler: true`, `cacheComponents: true`
- `next/font/google` (Geist)
- BetterAuth with JWT, admin, passkey, 2FA, Polar plugins
- Sentry (server + edge configs, tunnel route `/monitoring`)
- PostHog client analytics + `/ingest` rewrite
- `@vercel/analytics` (must be replaced)
- **Not used:** ISR, `next/image`, streaming, Edge runtime, parallel/intercepting routes

**Bundle-size risk (the headline)**
Workers Paid plan caps worker size at **10 MB gzipped**. Ballpark current bundle pressure:

| Dep | Approx. impact |
|---|---|
| Next.js + React 19 runtime | ~1–2 MB |
| Sentry (`@sentry/nextjs`, server + edge + tunnel) | ~1–2 MB |
| `posthog-js` | ~1.3 MB |
| `@polar-sh/sdk` | ~0.5–1 MB |
| Radix UI (13 components) + `lucide-react` + `@tanstack/react-table` | ~0.5–1 MB |
| App code (~10.7k LOC TS) | ~0.5 MB |

Estimated total: **5–7 MB gzipped**. Feasible but tight. Mitigations if over the cap:
1. Trim Sentry to tunnel-only (drop client SDK); or move to a lighter error tracker.
2. Replace `posthog-js` with a minimal beacon wrapper.
3. Replace `@polar-sh/sdk` with direct HTTP calls where used.
4. Verify React Compiler output isn't inflating client bundles.

**Compatibility risks to verify before committing**
1. OpenNext support for `reactCompiler` + `cacheComponents` on Next.js 16 (both relatively new).
2. Sentry `sentry.server.config.ts` + `sentry.edge.config.ts` must collapse into a single Workers runtime path.
3. BetterAuth session cookies under custom Worker domain (SameSite, Secure, domain).
4. Polar webhook signature verification using Workers `crypto` (no `node:crypto` specifics).
5. PostHog `/ingest` rewrite behaviour on Workers (rewrite target reachability, header passthrough).

**Migration surface**
- `apps/web/next.config.ts` — may need OpenNext-aware config; reconcile rewrites
- `apps/web/package.json` — add `@opennextjs/cloudflare`, remove `@vercel/analytics`
- Build pipeline — add `opennextjs-cloudflare build` + `wrangler deploy` to Turborepo
- `apps/web/wrangler.jsonc` — new file: bindings (Neon secret), assets, compatibility flags
- Sentry config — collapse edge + server into single runtime config
- `vercel.json` / Vercel project — decommission after cutover

**Out of scope**
- `apps/api` (separate tracks: #22 for SST, #35 for Vercel).
- Data-layer changes (`apps/web` already talks to Hono via JWT — unchanged).
- Auth provider changes.

**Relationship to other issues**
- Complements #22 (SST for Cloudflare): if this migration ships, SST would manage the web Worker too.
- Inverse of #35 (API → Vercel): pursuing this issue reinforces Cloudflare as the target platform; pursuing #35 obsoletes this one.

**Estimated effort:** 20–40 hours — dominated by bundle-size iteration and compatibility verification (OpenNext + Sentry + PostHog + Polar against Next 16 features). Budget extra time for shadow deploy + DNS cutover.

title: Expand ShipRadar reach to non-developers with email notifications and curated repo bundles
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
ShipRadar currently requires GitHub familiarity to get value from it — users need to know repo slugs, understand stars/forks, and live in Telegram or Discord. This blocks the PM, ops, journalist, and analyst personas who would benefit from release tracking but don't orient around GitHub.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Two features that address the highest-leverage gaps for non-developer adoption:

**1. Email notification channel with verification**

The existing notification layer already uses a clean `channelTypeSchema` enum + switch pattern, so email slots in without restructuring anything. Key additions:
- Extend the channel enum and add `emailConfigSchema` (`{ email: string }`) in `packages/database/src/schema/user-channels.ts`
- New `email.service.ts` in `apps/api/src/services/` using Resend (works natively on Cloudflare Workers, has a generous free tier)
- New `case "email"` handler in `notification.service.ts`
- Settings UI entry alongside Telegram/Discord

A verification flow is required before activating the channel (confirm link → `verified` bool + token table) to prevent misuse. Estimated effort: ~4 days including verification.

**2. Preset repository bundles for onboarding**

Rather than building a full collections/tagging system upfront, ship a static JSON of curated repo sets (e.g. `ai-sdks`, `design-tools`, `self-hosted-apps`) and surface them as "Start with a preset" cards on the empty-state dashboard. One click subscribes the user to all repos in the bundle and tags each via a `tags text[]` column on `user_repos`.

This gives non-developers a guided entry point without needing to know GitHub repo names. No tag CRUD UI in v1 — bundles are read-only presets. Estimated effort: ~3 days.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

- Full collections system with `collections` + `collection_repos` tables — more flexible but HIGH effort and not needed for the initial non-dev use case.
- Webhooks as the email substitute — too technical; non-developers won't configure them.

**Additional context**

Recommended sequencing:
1. Email channel + verification (unlocks PM/ops persona, highest conversion leverage)
2. Preset bundles + empty-state onboarding cards (lowers the floor for non-GitHub-native users)
3. Marketing page variant targeting "track the tools your team depends on"
4. Full collections/tags system only if users ask for custom grouping

Target personas: product managers monitoring competitor tool releases, tech journalists tracking tooling drops, IT/security ops watching upstream patches, designers tracking design-system repos.

title: Evaluate migrating apps/api from Cloudflare Workers to Vercel
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The API Worker (`apps/api`) runs on Cloudflare Workers with Workers-specific primitives (Durable Objects, Workflows, KV, Workers AI, Rate Limiting). The web app (`apps/web`) already runs on Vercel. Consolidating the API onto Vercel would reduce platform surface area and simplify ops — at the cost of rewriting the Workers-specific pieces.

This issue captures the migration inventory so the trade-off against #22 (SST-managed Cloudflare) is explicit.

**Current Cloudflare primitives in use**
- Hono Worker, webhook-based Telegram bot (Grammy)
- Durable Object `STATS` — single-instance counters (2 keys: `notifications_sent`, `releases_notified`)
- Workflow `RELEASE_CHECK_WORKFLOW` — 15-min release check with per-step retries (GitHub 5×, AI 2×, Telegram 3×)
- KV namespaces: `REPOS`, `NOTIFICATIONS`, `CACHE`, `CHANNELS`
- Workers AI: `@cf/meta/llama-3-8b-instruct` with JSON-schema structured output
- Rate Limiter: 20 req / 60s per user on write routes
- Cron: `*/15 * * * *`
- Custom domain: `api.releasewatch.dev`
- `ctx.waitUntil`, `blockConcurrencyWhile`, `@sentry/cloudflare`

**Proposed Vercel stack**

| Cloudflare | Vercel |
|---|---|
| Worker (Hono) | Vercel Functions on Fluid Compute (Node 24 LTS) |
| Durable Object `STATS` | Upstash Redis (`INCR` / `GET`) |
| Workflow `RELEASE_CHECK_WORKFLOW` | Vercel Workflow DevKit |
| KV namespaces (4) | Upstash Redis (Vercel Marketplace) |
| Workers AI | Vercel AI Gateway + AI SDK `generateObject` |
| Rate Limiter | `@upstash/ratelimit` |
| Cron | Vercel Cron Jobs (via `vercel.ts`) |
| `ctx.waitUntil` | `waitUntil` from `@vercel/functions` |
| `@sentry/cloudflare` | `@sentry/node` |
| Custom domain | Vercel custom domain |
| Neon HTTP driver | Unchanged |

**Open questions / risks**
1. Does Vercel Workflow DevKit's `step.do(..., { retry })` cover the exponential + constant + per-step-timeout combos in `apps/api/src/workflows/release-check.ts`?
2. Which AI Gateway provider matches `@cf/meta/llama-3-8b-instruct` for quality + cost? Needs sample-release benchmarking.
3. Vercel Workflow DevKit pricing at ~50 repos × 96 runs/day × N steps — check against current zero-ish Workers cost.
4. Latency impact of losing `smart_placement` on Telegram sends.
5. Grammy Telegram webhook re-registration during cutover (briefly double-delivered or dropped messages).

**Out of scope**
- `apps/web` (already on Vercel).
- `packages/database` (Neon HTTP driver — already cross-platform).

**Relationship to #22**
Mutually exclusive. If this issue is pursued, #22 is closed as "not planned". If #22 is pursued, this issue is closed or kept as "if we ever migrate".

**Estimated effort:** 30–50 hours including benchmarking the AI model swap and shadow-running the workflow before cutover.

---

## ⚠️ Blocker: Vercel Hobby plan cron limit

Migration code is complete on branch `35-evaluate-migrating-appsapi-from-cloudflare-workers-to-vercel`, but cutover is blocked:

- **Hobby plan**: cron jobs run at most **once per day** — no sub-daily schedules
- **Required**: `*/15 * * * *` (every 15 minutes)
- **Pro plan** (~$20/mo) is required for arbitrary sub-daily schedules

### Options
1. **Upgrade to Pro** — unlocks `*/15` natively
2. **External scheduler on Hobby** — GitHub Actions `schedule`, Upstash QStash, or cron-job.org pings `/api/cron/start-check`
3. **Revert migration** — keep `apps/api` on Cloudflare Workers and pursue #22 instead

Parked until a decision is made.

title: Set up preview environment for API with OAuth Proxy support
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, there's no way to test API changes in pull request previews. Web previews are deployed by Vercel, but the API must be deployed to production to test changes, which prevents proper full-stack testing before merging.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Set up a preview API environment that automatically deploys on pull requests, enabling full-stack testing before production deployment.

The implementation involves three phases:

**Phase 1: Code Changes**
Add OAuth Proxy to BetterAuth for preview authentication, update CORS to accept Vercel preview origins, add deployment scripts to package.json, configure Wrangler environment blocks for preview and production, and regenerate TypeScript types.

**Phase 2: Infrastructure**
Create preview KV namespaces (REPOS, NOTIFICATIONS, CACHE, CHANNELS), set up preview Hyperdrive connection, update Wrangler config with resource IDs, and configure secrets for the preview environment.

**Phase 3: CI/CD**
Create GitHub Actions workflow to automatically deploy preview API on pull requests and production API on main branch merges. Configure Vercel preview environment to use the preview API URL.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
1. **Production API only**: Web previews call production API - simpler setup but risky, can't test API changes before merging
2. **Manual deployment**: Deploy preview API manually for each PR - not scalable and defeats the purpose of automated PR previews

**Additional context**
This setup uses BetterAuth's OAuth Proxy plugin to handle authentication in previews, Cloudflare Workers environment blocks for isolated preview and production environments, Turborepo for monorepo task execution, and GitHub Actions for automatic deployment.

Expected outcome: PR previews work with OAuth authentication, preview API is deployed automatically on pull requests, and full-stack testing is possible before production deployment.

title: Add auto-publishing setup for repository owners to publish to multiple platforms
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Repository owners currently need to manually publish releases to different platforms, which can be time-consuming and error-prone when managing multiple distribution channels.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Provide repository owners and collaborators with the ability to configure auto-publishing to different platforms (e.g., npm, GitHub Releases, Docker Hub, etc.) when a new release is detected. This could include:
- Configuration UI in the dashboard to enable/disable auto-publishing per platform
- Platform-specific authentication/credentials management
- Automatic triggers when new GitHub releases are created
- Publishing status tracking and notifications

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- Manual publishing workflows using CI/CD pipelines
- Third-party automation tools like GitHub Actions
- Custom scripts for each platform

**Additional context**
This feature would streamline the release process and ensure consistent publishing across multiple platforms, reducing manual work for repository maintainers.

title: Add support for additional notification channels (Slack, Email, Messages, X)
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, ReleaseWatch only supports Telegram and Discord for notifications. Users who prefer other communication platforms cannot receive release updates through their preferred channels.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add support for additional notification channels:
- **Slack** - Webhook integration for team workspaces
- **Email** - Direct email notifications for individual users
- **Messages** - Apple Messages/iMessage support
- **X (Twitter)** - Direct messages or mentions for updates

Each channel should follow the same pattern as the existing Telegram/Discord integrations:
- Store channel configuration in the database
- Send formatted release summaries with AI-generated content
- Allow users to manage subscriptions per channel
- Support both individual and team/workspace notifications

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- Using third-party integration services (Zapier, IFTTT) - but native support provides better user experience
- Supporting only one additional channel at a time - but users may want multiple options
- RSS feeds as a universal alternative - but lacks the rich formatting and AI summaries

**Additional context**
This is a future enhancement to expand the platform's reach and accommodate different user preferences. Implementation can be phased:
1. Start with Slack (high demand in developer teams)
2. Add Email (universal accessibility)
3. Implement Messages and X as requested

Each integration will require:
- API client setup (similar to existing Grammy bot for Telegram)
- Database schema updates for channel preferences
- Frontend UI for channel configuration
- Webhook handlers (for Slack, Email services)

title: Implement SST for multi-environment infrastructure management
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The project uses a single `apps/api/wrangler.jsonc` with hardcoded production resource IDs (KV namespaces, Durable Object, Workflow, Rate Limiter, custom domain). This makes it difficult to safely manage multiple environments (dev, staging, production) without risking accidental changes to production resources during development. After releases, changes cannot be tested in isolated environments before deploying to production.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Adopt [SST](https://sst.dev) to manage Cloudflare resources across dev, staging, and production stages:

- Add an `infra/` package to the monorepo with `sst.config.ts`
- Use SST's first-class Cloudflare components where available:
  - `sst.cloudflare.Worker` for the API Worker
  - `sst.cloudflare.Kv` for the 4 KV namespaces
  - `sst.cloudflare.Ai` for the AI binding
  - `sst.cloudflare.Cron` for the 15-minute schedule
  - `sst.cloudflare.binding` for secrets / plain text
- Declare resources without native SST components (Durable Objects, Workflows, Rate Limiting) via the raw Cloudflare provider resources SST exposes, inside the same `sst.config.ts`
- Use SST stages for dev/staging/prod (e.g. `sst deploy --stage dev`, `--stage staging`, `--stage prod`)
- Import existing production resources into SST state using the `import:` property
- Integrate with Turborepo for build dependencies
- Add GitHub Actions workflows for per-stage deployments

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- **Wrangler environments**: Native but verbose given the number of bindings across environments.

**Additional context**
Resources to manage per environment (authoritative, from `apps/api/wrangler.jsonc`):
- 4 KV namespaces: `REPOS`, `NOTIFICATIONS`, `CACHE`, `CHANNELS`
- 1 Durable Object: `STATS` (class `Stats`, SQLite-backed)
- 1 Workflow: `RELEASE_CHECK_WORKFLOW`
- 1 AI binding: `AI` (remote)
- 1 Rate Limiter: `RATE_LIMITER` (20 req / 60s)
- 1 Cron trigger: `*/15 * * * *`
- 1 Custom domain: `api.releasewatch.dev`
- Worker runtime: `nodejs_compat`, smart placement, observability enabled
- Secrets: `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `DASHBOARD_API_KEY`, `JWKS_URL`, `DATABASE_URL`, plus optional `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL`, `POSTHOG_API_KEY`

**SST readiness per Cloudflare resource**

| Cloudflare resource | Current binding | SST support |
|---|---|---|
| Worker | `apps/api` | ✅ `sst.cloudflare.Worker` |
| KV namespace | `REPOS`, `NOTIFICATIONS`, `CACHE`, `CHANNELS` | ✅ `sst.cloudflare.Kv` |
| Workers AI | `AI` | ✅ `sst.cloudflare.Ai` |
| Cron Trigger | `*/15 * * * *` | ✅ `sst.cloudflare.Cron` |
| Custom domain | `api.releasewatch.dev` | ✅ `sst.cloudflare.Worker` `domain` option |
| Secrets / plain text | `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `DATABASE_URL`, etc. | ✅ `sst.cloudflare.binding` |
| Durable Object | `STATS` (class `Stats`, SQLite) | ⚠️ No native component |
| Workflow | `RELEASE_CHECK_WORKFLOW` | ⚠️ No native component |
| Rate Limiting | `RATE_LIMITER` (20 / 60s) | ⚠️ No native component |

Native coverage: 6 of 9 resource types. The 3 gaps carry the bulk of the implementation risk and should be prototyped first.

Estimated effort: 8–10 hours for complete setup including import of existing production resources.

> **Conditional on platform direction:** This work is subject to whether the API eventually migrates to Vercel. If the Worker moves to Vercel Functions / Fluid Compute, the Cloudflare-specific SST setup proposed here would be replaced with Vercel-native infrastructure (e.g. `vercel.ts`), and this issue should be closed or rescoped accordingly.

title: Show watcher count for tracked repos (social proof)
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Users don't know if they're the only one tracking a particular repo or if it's popular among other ReleaseWatch users. Social proof can help users discover popular repos and feel part of a community.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Show how many users are tracking each repo:
- Add watcher count to `GET /repos` response
- Use batch query to avoid N+1 performance issues
- Only display count when > 1 user (privacy: don't expose single-user tracking)
- Optional: Cache counts in KV (5-minute TTL)
- Optional: Show "Popular" badge for repos with 50+ watchers

**Implementation details**
Files to modify:
- `apps/api/src/index.ts` - Add watcher count aggregation query
- `apps/api/src/services/kv.service.ts` - Optional caching
- `apps/web/src/components/subscriptions/subscription-list.tsx` - Display count

**Additional context**
Display format: "12 users tracking" or "🔥 Popular" for high counts.
Invalidate cache on repo add/remove for accuracy.

title: Pause repo tracking
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Users may want to temporarily stop receiving notifications for certain repos without completely removing them from their tracked list. For example, during busy periods or when a repo is releasing too frequently.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add the ability to pause/unpause repo tracking:
- Add `paused` boolean column to `user_repos` table
- Add `PATCH /repos/:id/pause` API endpoint to toggle pause state
- Modify notification workflow to skip paused repos
- Add pause/play toggle UI in subscription list
- When unpausing, skip to latest release (don't send missed notifications)

**Implementation details**
Files to modify:
- `packages/database/src/schema/user-repos.ts` - Add `paused` column
- `apps/api/src/index.ts` - Add PATCH endpoint
- `apps/api/src/workflows/release-check.ts` - Filter paused repos
- `apps/web/src/components/subscriptions/subscription-list.tsx` - UI toggle

**Additional context**
On unpause, update `lastNotifiedTag` to current latest to avoid notification spam from missed releases.

title: Migrate all API routes from Next.js to Hono with JWT authentication
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, the application has 12 API routes split between Next.js (apps/web) and Hono (apps/api). Several Next.js routes simply proxy requests to Hono, adding unnecessary latency and complexity. This architecture creates:
- Extra network hop (Browser → Vercel → Cloudflare)
- Increased cold start latency from Vercel serverless functions
- Duplicate authentication logic across both platforms
- Additional maintenance overhead for proxy routes

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Migrate all API routes to Hono (Cloudflare Workers) and have the frontend communicate directly with the Hono API using JWT authentication via BetterAuth's built-in JWT plugin.

Implementation approach:
1. Enable BetterAuth's JWT plugin to automatically create `/api/auth/token` and `/api/auth/jwks` endpoints
2. Create JWT validation middleware in Hono using JWKS (asymmetric key verification)
3. Migrate routes in phases:
   - Phase 1: Dashboard routes (already proxied)
   - Phase 2: Telegram integration routes (already proxied)
   - Phase 3: Subscriptions routes (new implementation)
   - Phase 4: Admin routes (user management, activity logs)
4. Update frontend components to use direct API client with automatic token refresh
5. Remove legacy proxy routes and environment variables

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- **Keep current proxy pattern**: Simpler short-term but maintains latency and complexity
- **Bearer plugin instead of JWT**: Uses session tokens directly but requires database lookup for validation (slower)
- **Move admin routes only**: Partial migration leaves architecture split and inconsistent

**Additional context**
- BetterAuth JWT plugin uses asymmetric keys (EdDSA) via JWKS - no shared secrets needed
- Token lifetime: 1 hour with automatic refresh via session validation
- Frontend can call Cloudflare edge directly, reducing latency from ~250ms to near-zero
- Admin authorization via JWT role claim (no database lookup needed)
- Detailed implementation plan available in `~/.claude/plans/bright-humming-petal.md`

Routes to migrate:
- `/api/subscriptions` (GET/POST)
- `/api/subscriptions/[id]` (DELETE)
- `/api/dashboard/releases`, `/api/dashboard/stats`
- `/api/integrations/telegram/status`, `/api/integrations/telegram/generate`
- `/api/admin/users`, `/api/admin/users/[id]`, `/api/admin/users/[id]/ban`
- `/api/admin/activity`, `/api/admin/stats`

title: Build Next.js web dashboard
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Users need a web interface to manage subscriptions and notification channels, especially for non-Telegram workflows.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Create `apps/web/` with Next.js 15:
- **Auth**: NextAuth.js v5 with GitHub + Google OAuth
- **Pages**:
  - Landing page (public)
  - Dashboard home (releases feed)
  - Subscriptions management (add/remove repos)
  - Channels management (link Telegram, add Discord webhook)
  - Account settings
- **UI**: shadcn/ui components, Tailwind CSS
- **API Client**: Communicate with Cloudflare Worker API

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- Remix on Cloudflare Pages (less mature ecosystem)
- Astro (not ideal for authenticated apps)
- Keep Telegram-only (limits growth)

**Additional context**
Deploy to Vercel. Use `@release-watch/types` package for shared types. This is the main user-facing interface for the multi-channel system.

title: Build notification abstraction layer
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The workflow currently hardcodes `sendTelegramNotification()`. To support multiple channels, we need an abstraction layer.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Create `src/services/notification.service.ts`:
- `notifyUser(channels, payload, env)` - Dispatch to all enabled channels
- Support Telegram, Discord (more channels in future)
- Return results per channel (success/failure)

Update `src/workflows/release-check.ts`:
- Support both new user flow (multi-channel) and legacy Telegram flow
- Filter out linked chats from legacy flow (avoid double-notify)
- Use the new notification dispatcher

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- Keep channel-specific logic in workflow (not scalable)
- Pub/sub pattern (overkill for current scale)

**Additional context**
This is the core abstraction enabling multi-channel notifications. Must handle failures gracefully (one channel failing shouldn't block others).

title: Add Discord webhook notification support
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Users want to receive release notifications in Discord servers, not just Telegram.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Implement Discord webhook integration:
- Create `src/services/discord.service.ts`
- `sendDiscordNotification(webhookUrl, payload)` - Send rich embeds
- `validateDiscordWebhook(url)` - Validate webhook before saving
- Format notifications with embeds (colors by category, highlights, breaking changes)

Users enter their Discord webhook URL in the web dashboard.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- Discord bot (requires bot hosting, more complex setup)
- Plain text messages (less rich, worse UX)

**Additional context**
Discord is the first new channel after Telegram. Design this service to be easily extensible for future channels (email, webhooks).

title: Implement Telegram account linking
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Existing Telegram bot users need a way to link their Telegram account to the new web dashboard and import their subscriptions.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Implement a code-based linking flow:
1. User clicks "Link Telegram" on web dashboard
2. Dashboard generates 6-character code (10 min TTL)
3. User sends `/link ABC123` to Telegram bot
4. Bot validates code, creates `telegram:{chatId} → userId` mapping
5. Bot adds Telegram channel to user's channels
6. Optionally import existing subscriptions

Add `/link` command to the Telegram bot (`src/bot/index.ts`).

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- OAuth-based linking (complex, Telegram doesn't support standard OAuth)
- Manual chatId entry (poor UX, security risk)

**Additional context**
This enables existing users to transition to the web dashboard while keeping their Telegram notifications working.

title: Add API endpoints for user and subscription management
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The Next.js web dashboard needs API endpoints to manage users, subscriptions, and notification channels.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add REST API endpoints to the Cloudflare Worker:
- `POST /api/users` - Create/update user from OAuth
- `GET/POST/DELETE /api/users/:userId/subscriptions` - Manage subscriptions
- `GET/POST/DELETE /api/users/:userId/channels` - Manage notification channels
- `POST /api/link/telegram/generate` - Generate Telegram linking code
- `GET /api/link/telegram/status/:userId` - Check link status

Auth: Shared API key via `X-API-Key` header between Vercel and Cloudflare.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- Direct KV access from Next.js (not possible, KV is Cloudflare-specific)
- GraphQL API (overkill for this use case)

**Additional context**
These endpoints will be called by the Next.js dashboard. Extend `src/index.ts` with new routes.

title: Redesign KV schema for multi-channel support
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The current KV schema is tightly coupled to Telegram (`chat:{chatId}`). To support multiple notification channels (Discord, email, webhooks), we need a user-centric schema.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Redesign KV structure to:
- Introduce `user:{userId}` as the central entity
- Add `channels:{userId}` to store multiple notification channels per user
- Change `subs:{userId}` for user subscriptions
- Use `notified:{userId}:{repo}` for deduplication (not per-channel)
- Add `telegram:{chatId} → userId` mapping for account linking
- Keep legacy `chat:{chatId}` for backward compatibility

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- Migrate to D1 database (deferred, staying with KV for now)
- Keep per-channel deduplication (would create unnecessary notification spam)

**Additional context**
This is a foundation for multi-channel notifications. Must maintain backward compatibility with existing Telegram-only users.

title: Set up Turborepo monorepo structure
description:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
As we expand to support a web dashboard alongside the Cloudflare Worker, we need a better way to organize the codebase and share code (especially TypeScript types) between the worker and the Next.js app.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Convert the existing flat structure to a Turborepo monorepo with:
- `apps/worker/` - Existing Cloudflare Worker code
- `apps/web/` - New Next.js dashboard (placeholder for now)
- `packages/types/` - Shared TypeScript types used by both apps

This will enable:
- Shared types between worker and web
- Atomic changes across both apps in one PR
- Unified tooling (Biome, tsconfig)
- Turborepo caching for fast builds

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- Keep separate repos (harder to maintain, duplicate types)
- Use pnpm/bun workspaces only (less build optimization)
- Use Nx (more complex than needed)

**Additional context**
This is Phase 0 of the web dashboard implementation. The monorepo setup must be completed and verified (worker still deploys correctly) before proceeding with other features.

title: Decision: no native mobile app — deliver via Telegram/Discord, prefer PWA
description:
Decision: do not build a native mobile app for the foreseeable future. Deliver notifications via existing channels (Telegram/Discord); if a mobile surface is ever wanted, prefer a PWA over native.

Rationale (market research, 2026-06): No competitor in the release-tracking category ships a native app — NewReleases.io (category leader, 13 ecosystems, paid team tier), GitWatchman, and self-hosted github-release-monitor all deliver via email + chat. The core value (release push) is already a best-in-class mobile experience via Telegram/Discord. Release-checking is a glance, not a session — poor fit for native retention economics; developers live on desktop. Native would only make sense for a non-developer pivot (see GH #36). A PWA (installable + Web Push) is the pragmatic middle, reuses the Next.js app, rides the BetterAuth session, and needs no JWT bridge.

Architecture implication: this unblocks consolidating apps/api into apps/web and dropping the JWT/JWKS bridge (see linked consolidation issue). If native is ever committed to, BetterAuth's dormant JWT plugin can be re-enabled inside the consolidated app.
notes: Unblocks release-watch-k9p (consolidate apps/api into apps/web, drop JWT bridge). Decision rationale backed by market research; if a native app is ever revisited, re-enable BetterAuth's dormant JWT plugin inside the consolidated app rather than resurrecting a separate service.

title: Research X/Twitter notification channel
description: Research feasibility for X/Twitter notifications before implementation. Evaluate API access, auth requirements, DM versus mention behavior, pricing/rate limits, policy constraints, and whether the channel is worth showing as coming soon.
acceptance_criteria: A short design note documents API feasibility, costs, auth flow, delivery mode, policy risks, and a recommendation to implement, defer, or remove from coming-soon UI.

title: Add RSS feed notification channel
description: Add RSS feed support as a universal low-friction way to consume release updates. Determine whether feeds are user-level, repo-level, or both, and implement secure feed URLs with recent release entries.
acceptance_criteria: Users can access an RSS feed for their tracked release updates; feed entries include repo, version, summary, and release URL; private/user-specific feeds use unguessable tokens; integrations UI exposes the feed clearly.

title: Polish pricing and marketing UI with HeroUI patterns
description:
GitHub: https://github.com/ruchernchong/release-watch/issues/48

Refine pricing and marketing screens after primitives are migrated so they match HeroUI Pro design patterns.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Pricing cards use HeroUI components and semantic variants.
- Marketing pages no longer depend on shadcn primitives.
- Desktop and mobile layouts remain polished and readable.

title: Remove shadcn/ui files and unused dependencies
description:
GitHub: https://github.com/ruchernchong/release-watch/issues/45

Remove shadcn/ui generated files and dependencies once all migration work is complete.

Acceptance Criteria:
- rg '@/components/ui' apps/web/src returns no app imports.
- Unused shadcn/Radix dependencies are removed from apps/web/package.json.
- pnpm lint, pnpm typecheck, and pnpm build pass.

title: Add Telegram Better Auth docs, security hardening, and test coverage
description: Capture known Telegram limitations, secure token/signature validation requirements, and add tests/docs for the custom Better Auth integration path before enabling production use.

title: Add Telegram auth integration path with Better Auth
description: Track and revisit Telegram authentication options using Better Auth. Telegram is not currently natively supported in core, so this work defines a practical integration path and fallback strategy for later implementation.

title: Add Pushover notification channel
description: Add Pushover as a later personal push notification channel for power users. Store user/app token configuration, send formatted release summaries, and support enable/disable/remove management.
acceptance_criteria: Users can configure Pushover credentials; release summaries are delivered through Pushover; users can enable, disable, and remove the channel; invalid credentials produce actionable errors.
